stem: The length of the edge of the base of a right square prism is 5 units. The volume of the prism is 1,500 cubic units. What is the height, in units, of the prism?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The base of a right square prism is a square with side length 5, so the base area is 52 = 25 square units. The volume V of a prism equals the base area times the height: V = Bh. Substituting V = 1,500 and B = 25 yields 1,500 = 25h, so h = 60 units. Choices A, B, and D are incorrect and result from formula or arithmetic errors.
